response.justification: Bogdan Szabo does not meet the minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience, yet the candidate's skills section omits C++ entirely, with only a ~6-month stint at National Instruments involving C/C++ components and a bachelor's thesis project using C++. His career has been almost entirely JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js. While he demonstrates solid software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and some exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby, there is no evidence of sustained cloud platform engineering, infrastructure leadership, or the depth of systems-level expertise this role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second (Dunnhumby)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development practiced across multiple roles
Broad polyglot background with exposure to Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, and low-latency async I/O systems
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments + a thesis project) versus the 8-year minimum requirement — a hard disqualifier
No demonstrated technical leadership of cloud or infrastructure teams; leadership experience is limited to mentoring workshops and open-source maintainership
No evidence of owning or architecting large-scale cloud compute or infrastructure platforms; cloud exposure is peripheral (AWS CDK for pricing, Kubernetes for personal project deployment)
